title: Add React hooks for real-time subscriptions
markdown:
## Goal

Create React hooks that wrap the WebSocketClient subscription API, giving React developers a declarative way to subscribe to real-time updates from a B3nd node.

## Background

G1-A5-02 added `subscribe()` to `WebSocketClient`. React apps need hooks that manage subscription lifecycle (subscribe on mount, unsubscribe on unmount, handle reconnection, provide loading/error states).

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `useSubscription(prefix: string, options?)` hook:
  - Subscribes to the given URI prefix on mount
  - Unsubscribes on unmount or when prefix changes
  - Returns `{ data: T | null, uri: string | null, loading: boolean, error: string | null }`
  - Updates `data` and `uri` on each notification
  - Options include: `enabled?: boolean`, `onUpdate?: (uri, data) => void`
- [ ] `useSubscriptionList(prefix: string, options?)` hook:
  - Like `useSubscription` but accumulates notifications into a list
  - Returns `{ items: Array<{ uri: string, data: T }>, loading: boolean, error: string | null, clear: () => void }`
  - Options include: `maxItems?: number`, `enabled?: boolean`
- [ ] Hooks handle client not being available (loading state until client connects)
- [ ] Hooks clean up subscriptions properly on unmount (no memory leaks)
- [ ] Example usage documented in hook file comments

## Implementation Guide

1. Read `apps/b3nd-web-rig/src/hooks/` to understand existing hook patterns
2. Check if there is an existing B3nd client context/provider in the web rig (search for WebSocketClient usage)
3. Create `apps/b3nd-web-rig/src/hooks/useSubscription.ts`:
   - Use `useEffect` to subscribe on mount / prefix change
   - Use `useState` for data, uri, loading, error
   - Use `useRef` for subscription handle to avoid stale closures
   - Call `subscription.unsubscribe()` in cleanup
4. Create `apps/b3nd-web-rig/src/hooks/useSubscriptionList.ts`:
   - Similar to useSubscription but accumulates items in state array
   - Provide `maxItems` cap to prevent unbounded memory growth
   - Provide `clear()` to reset the list
5. If no client context exists, create `apps/b3nd-web-rig/src/hooks/useB3ndClient.ts` that provides a React context for the WebSocketClient instance

## Files to Create/Modify

- `apps/b3nd-web-rig/src/hooks/useSubscription.ts` -- new file: single-value subscription hook
- `apps/b3nd-web-rig/src/hooks/useSubscriptionList.ts` -- new file: list-accumulating subscription hook
- `apps/b3nd-web-rig/src/hooks/useB3ndClient.ts` -- new file (if needed): client context provider

## Testing

Manual testing in the web rig:
```bash
cd apps/b3nd-web-rig && npm run dev
```

Use the SDK inspector or a test component that subscribes to a prefix and displays incoming notifications.

## Dependencies

- **Depends on**: G1-A5-02 (WebSocketClient subscribe API)
- **Blocks**: Nothing
tasksExcerpt:
- [ ] Design WebSocket subscription protocol (server-push for URI prefix changes)
- [ ] Implement server-side: node tracks subscriptions, pushes matching messages
- [ ] Implement `WebSocketClient` subscribe mode (beyond current request/response)
- [ ] Add React hooks for subscriptions (`useSubscription(prefix)`)
